title: Add other vehicle details, multiple witnesses, and video uploader to main report form

requirements.text: Add an 'Other Vehicle' section to the AccidentReportForm that collects driver contact details (name, phone, email, address), insurance information (insurer name, policy number, claim reference), and vehicle details (make, model, year, colour, licence plate, MOT/registration info) — mirroring the structure and field style of the existing vehicle section already in the form.

quotes: For 1 just the driver contact details, insurance info, and vehicle details like the other section we did already

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Other Vehicle' section appears in the AccidentReportForm with a clearly labelled heading
Driver contact fields include: full name, phone number, email, and address
Insurance fields include: insurer name, policy number, and claim reference
Vehicle detail fields include: make, model, year, colour, and licence plate — matching the layout and styling of the existing vehicle section
All new fields are persisted to the backend AccidentReport type and returned when a report is fetched
New report detail view renders the other vehicle section with all submitted data

requirements.text: Add a dynamic multiple-witness section to the AccidentReportForm that allows users to add, edit, and remove individual witness entries. Each witness entry must include: full name, contact information (phone and/or email), and a free-text statement field.

quotes: Yes multiple witnesses is good, including names, contact info, statements etc.

acceptanceCriteria: Users can add multiple witnesses using an 'Add Witness' button
Each witness entry has fields for: full name, phone number, email, and a statement text area
Users can remove individual witness entries
Witness data is stored as an array on the backend AccidentReport type
Submitted witness entries are displayed in the report detail view
At least one witness entry can be added and removed without error

requirements.text: Add a video file uploader to the AccidentReportForm that accepts any relevant video footage from the user's device. The uploader should support common video formats (MP4, MOV, AVI, WebM), display the filename and a remove option after selection, and store the video as a blob on the backend report.
acceptanceCriteria: A video upload field is present in the AccidentReportForm with a clearly labelled heading
The uploader accepts MP4, MOV, AVI, and WebM file formats
After a file is selected, its filename is displayed with an option to remove it
Video data is persisted to the backend AccidentReport type
The report detail view indicates when a video has been uploaded and allows viewing or downloading it

requirements.text: Move the injury photo uploader (InjuryPhotoUpload / InjuryAnalysisPanel) to a prominent position on the main new-report page (NewReportPage / AccidentReportForm), ensuring it is visible without scrolling or clearly signposted near the top of the form.

quotes: Can you put it on the main page?

acceptanceCriteria: The injury photo uploader is rendered on the main report creation page
It appears near the top of the form or in a clearly prominent position
Uploading injury photos from this location triggers AI analysis as before
No duplicate injury uploader exists elsewhere on the page

constraints: The other vehicle section must use the same field style and UI patterns as the existing vehicle section in AccidentReportForm
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Real-time video streaming or playback analysis
AI analysis of uploaded video footage
Linking to external video hosting services (YouTube, cloud links)
Changes to the fault reference, grid view, or legal reference pages
